title: Identify Core OptiSweep Environment Components And Their Communication Path
summary: Use the training overview slide and transcript to identify the core components shown in the OptiSweep environment overview and confirm the communication roles described for UPS Chat and OptiSweep Software.
when_to_use: Use this reference procedure when reviewing the OptiSweep overview architecture from the training source to identify the labeled system components and restate the communication relationship described in the source between UPS Chat, OptiSweep Software, and connected building systems.
not_for: Do not use this runbook for configuration, commissioning, or troubleshooting of live communications.
Do not use this runbook to infer additional interfaces, protocols, or data flows beyond what the source explicitly states.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the training slide and transcript, identify the labeled components, and record the communication roles exactly as described by the source.

safety_notes: This is a reference procedure derived from a training overview segment rather than an operational control task.
Do not infer additional interfaces, protocols, or data flows beyond what the source explicitly states.
Escalate for clarification if a component or connection is not clearly labeled in the source material.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the training video segment or extracted slide

access_required: Access to the training video segment or extracted slide
Access to the transcript for the segment
system_preconditions: The overview architecture slide for training_video_day1 segment 0005 is available for review.

visual_references.description: Overview architecture slide showing UPS Chat, tote tipper or tote tip stations, Geek+ AGVs, hospital controller, OptiSweep Software, sorter, printer, and Aveva HMI, with connections referenced by the transcript.

steps.title: Open the overview slide and locate the labeled components
steps.purpose: Establish the set of core elements presented in the source overview.
steps.instruction: Open the training overview slide for the segment and locate the labeled components shown in the architecture diagram, including UPS Chat, tote tipper or tote tip stations, Geek+ AGVs, hospital controller, OptiSweep Software, sorter, printer, and Aveva HMI.
steps.expected_result: The labeled core elements on the overview slide are identified for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The listed components are visible and can be matched to labels on the overview diagram.
steps.failure_condition: One or more components cannot be located or the slide is not available or legible.

actions.label: Open overview slide
actions.exact_action: Open the training overview slide for the segment.

actions.label: Locate labeled components
actions.exact_action: Locate the labeled components shown in the architecture diagram, including UPS Chat, tote tipper or tote tip stations, Geek+ AGVs, hospital controller, OptiSweep Software, sorter, printer, and Aveva HMI.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The overview architecture slide showing the labeled core elements and their connections.

stop_or_escalate_if: A component expected from the source cannot be clearly identified on the slide.
The source material is unclear or not legible enough to confirm labels.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Overview slide shows UPS Chat, tote tipper/tote tip stations, Geek+ AGVs, hospital controller, OptiSweep Software, sorter, printer, and Aveva HMI; transcript also mentions scan tunnel.
Training slide showing the core OptiSweep system elements and how OptiSweep connects UPS Chat, tote handling equipment, AGVs, hospital controller, sorter, and HMI.

steps.title: Identify UPS Chat as the standard communication layer
steps.purpose: Confirm the communication role assigned to UPS Chat by the source.
steps.instruction: Identify UPS Chat on the diagram and note the transcript statement that UPS uses a standard communication method through all buildings and that sorters talk the same way through this chat.
steps.expected_result: UPS Chat is recognized as the standard communication layer described in the training source.
steps.healthy_condition: UPS Chat is identified on the diagram and its role is recorded using the source wording.
steps.failure_condition: UPS Chat cannot be located on the diagram or its role cannot be confirmed from the source.

actions.label: Locate UPS Chat
actions.exact_action: Identify UPS Chat on the diagram.

actions.label: Note communication role
actions.exact_action: Note the transcript statement that UPS uses a standard communication method through all buildings and that sorters talk the same way through this chat.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The UPS Chat label and its placement in the overview architecture slide.

stop_or_escalate_if: The diagram label for UPS Chat is unclear.
The communication role cannot be tied back to the supplied source evidence.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: UPS has a standard communication through all buildings; all the sorters talk the same way and it goes onto a chat.
UPS Chat is described as the standard communication method used across UPS buildings. The training segment says all sorters communicate the same way through this chat layer.

steps.title: Locate OptiSweep Software and note its integration role
steps.purpose: Confirm the role assigned to OptiSweep Software in the source overview.
steps.instruction: Locate OptiSweep Software on the diagram and note the source statement that OptiSweep software integrates everything so the connected systems talk and work as a team.
steps.expected_result: OptiSweep Software is identified as the integration layer described in the source.
steps.healthy_condition: OptiSweep Software is visible on the diagram and its integration role is recorded using source-supported wording.
steps.failure_condition: OptiSweep Software cannot be located or the integration role cannot be confirmed from the source.

actions.label: Locate OptiSweep Software
actions.exact_action: Locate OptiSweep Software on the diagram.

actions.label: Record integration role
actions.exact_action: Note the source statement that OptiSweep software integrates everything so the connected systems talk and work as a team.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The OptiSweep Software label and its relationship to the other components on the overview slide.

stop_or_escalate_if: The OptiSweep Software label is unclear on the source slide.
The integration role cannot be confirmed from the supplied transcript evidence.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Our OptiSweep software integrates everything, so everything talks and works as a team.
OptiSweep software is described as the integration layer for the system. In this training segment, it is the software that makes connected components talk and work together as a team.

steps.title: Verify the stated communication path for scan tunnel, tipper, and sorter
steps.purpose: Confirm which systems the source says must connect through UPS Chat.
steps.instruction: Use the transcript and diagram together to verify that communication with the scan tunnel, tipper, and sorter is described as requiring connection to UPS Chat.
steps.expected_result: The source-supported communication relationship is confirmed for the scan tunnel, tipper, and sorter.
steps.healthy_condition: The review confirms, based on the supplied source, that communication with the scan tunnel, tipper, and sorter is described as requiring connection to UPS Chat.
steps.failure_condition: The communication relationship for these systems cannot be confirmed from the supplied source material.

actions.label: Review transcript and diagram together
actions.exact_action: Use the transcript and diagram together to verify the described communication relationship.

actions.label: Confirm connected systems
actions.exact_action: Verify that communication with the scan tunnel, tipper, and sorter is described as requiring connection to UPS Chat.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The overview slide connections associated with UPS Chat and the connected equipment referenced by the transcript.

stop_or_escalate_if: The source does not clearly show or state the communication relationship for one of the listed systems.
Any interpretation would require inferring protocols or flows not explicitly stated.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The aligned transcript states that OptiSweep must connect to the chat to communicate with the scan tunnel, tipper, and sorter.
UPS has a standard communication through all buildings; all the sorters talk the same way and it goes onto a chat.

steps.title: Record the components and communication roles from the overview
steps.purpose: Create a source-grounded summary of what is part of the overview and what role each communication element plays.
steps.instruction: Record which components are presented as part of the OptiSweep environment overview and which communication role is assigned to UPS Chat versus OptiSweep Software.
steps.expected_result: A source-grounded summary is produced listing the overview components and distinguishing UPS Chat as the common communication layer from OptiSweep Software as the integration layer.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded summary matches the source-supported component list and communication roles without adding unsupported detail.
steps.failure_condition: The summary includes unsupported assumptions or cannot distinguish the roles from the source evidence.

actions.label: Record component list
actions.exact_action: Record which components are presented as part of the OptiSweep environment overview.

actions.label: Record communication roles
actions.exact_action: Record which communication role is assigned to UPS Chat versus OptiSweep Software.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The full overview architecture slide used to summarize the component list and communication roles.

stop_or_escalate_if: You cannot distinguish whether a statement is directly supported by the source.
A component or role assignment is ambiguous in the source material.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Overview slide shows UPS Chat, tote tipper/tote tip stations, Geek+ AGVs, hospital controller, OptiSweep Software, sorter, printer, and Aveva HMI; transcript also mentions scan tunnel.
UPS has a standard communication through all buildings; all the sorters talk the same way and it goes onto a chat.
Our OptiSweep software integrates everything, so everything talks and works as a team.

success_criteria: The user can identify the core components shown in the overview architecture slide.
The user can state, based on the source, that UPS Chat is the common communication layer.
The user can state, based on the source, that OptiSweep Software is the integration layer that makes connected systems work as a team.
The recorded summary does not add unsupported interfaces, protocols, or data flows.
healthy_conditions: The overview slide is available and legible.
The labeled components on the slide can be matched to the source-supported list.
UPS Chat and OptiSweep Software roles can be distinguished directly from the supplied source evidence.
The final notes remain limited to what the training source explicitly states.
failure_conditions: The overview slide or transcript is unavailable or unclear.
A listed component cannot be confidently identified from the source.
The communication relationship for scan tunnel, tipper, or sorter cannot be confirmed from the supplied evidence.
The reviewer must infer unsupported interfaces, protocols, or data flows to complete the summary.
escalation_guidance: Escalate for clarification if a component or connection is not clearly labeled in the source material.
Escalate if the source evidence is insufficient to distinguish the role of UPS Chat from the role of OptiSweep Software.
Do not proceed with assumptions when the source does not explicitly support a communication path or component relationship.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overview architecture slide showing the labeled core elements and their connections.
